pundits.name: Eric Wynalda
pundits.take: Let's set the record straight: Gayden is the only manager with a sliver of competence here in this fantasy pool. The rest of you, Zach, Gunner, and Devin, have clearly mistaken the World Cup for a schoolyard kickaround. Devin, I don't know what you were thinking, picking Norway and South Africa like your strategy was based on FIFA video game stats or favorite jersey colors. They got you zero points, mate! Meanwhile, Zach, banking on Czechia? That's a joke. Maybe you watched one YouTube highlight and thought they were contenders. Gayden at least knows how to pick winners with England and Turkey, but honestly, the rest of you need to take some notes before embarrassing yourselves on draft night again.

pundits.name: Landon Donovan
pundits.take:
Look, I get why people say Gayden is leading the pool because he "understands the game," but let's be honest, picking Korea Republic and Turkey in Tier 3 isn't exactly the work of a mastermind, is it? More like the football gods are smiling on him because he happened to watch a brief YouTube clip on "teams that might do well this year" and took it as gospel. 

Now, I feel for Devin, I really do, but when you draft Haiti in the fourth round, bless his heart, what conversation were you having with yourself? Maybe he got caught up in the moment or confused the World Cup with a geography bee. And Zach—come on, man—did you let someone else fill your roster? Czechia over the likes of more proven teams? Perhaps he picked them based on jersey color, thinking "blue and white might blend nicely on my fantasy dashboard." 

As for Gunner, watching him hold on to a single point like it's gold, you'd think he picked teams based solely on how cool their emblems look. With Canada and DR Congo coming up, we'll see if he's in it for the backup dancers or ready to step up to the main stage.

pundits.name: Clint Dempsey
pundits.take:
Man, look at ol' Gayden out here sittin' pretty on top with 6 points! Dude must've Googled "how to win fantasy soccer" before the draft 'cause he's puttin' in work with Korea Republic and Turkey. Meanwhile, Zach's tryin' to ride that Swiss train with 4 points, probably thinkin' chocolate and watch factories translate to goals. 

And Gunner, my man, did you pick DR Congo after you saw a cool flag on a T-shirt or somethin'? You got France, but only 1 point? That's tough, bro. Devin, c'mon now, zero points? I know you're backin' Spain and the USA, but it feels like you picked Haiti just 'cause you saw 'em score once in a YouTube highlight reel. Get it together, y'all!

pundits.name: Alexi Lalas
pundits.take:
Let me tell you something, Gayden, just because you’re riding high at the top with 6 points doesn’t mean you’re the king of this World Cup mountain. You’ve got Korea Republic and Turkey carrying your sorry selections, and while England might be saving you, remember it’s the grit from the 90s era that should be inspiring you, not the flashy tactics of today. Your lead is fragile, like a house of cards built on a windy day, just waiting for someone to come along and blow it down.

Zach, look at me. LOOK AT ME. Switzerland, really? You went all in because they had a nice run in a YouTube highlight you saw once, didn’t you? Picking Czechia like you were drafting a beer to guzzle rather than a team to rely on. Sitting in second place with 4 points might inflate your ego, but don’t forget, it’s your Brazil that’s doing the heavy lifting, and let’s face it, they can’t play all the matches for you.

And Devin, my word, you’re propping up the table on zero points! Zero! It’s as if you chose Bosnia and Haiti because you liked their jersey colors. Did you do any research, or did a magic eight ball dictate your picks
